etymology.description: semantic 豆(豆) (dòu, "bean") + phonetic 支(支) (zhī) -> fermented soybeans
etymology.interpretation: 豆(豆) (dòu, "bean") provides the context of beans, which is crucial as the character relates to fermented soybeans, a type of food product. 支(支) (zhī) is used for its phonetic contribution, associating the sound with the product type.

examples.chinese: 我特别喜欢吃豆豉(我特別喜歡吃豆豉)。
examples.english: I especially like eating fermented soybeans.

examples.chinese: 豆豉是一种常用于调味的食材(豆豉是一種常用於調味的食材)。
examples.pinyin: Dòuchǐ shì yī zhǒng cháng yòng yú tiáowèi de shícái.
examples.english: Fermented soybeans are an ingredient often used for seasoning.

examples.chinese: 他做的菜总是加一点豆豉(他做的菜總是加一點豆豉)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zuò de cài zǒngshì jiā yīdiǎn dòuchǐ.
examples.english: He always adds a little fermented soybeans to his dishes.
